guide.title: Use functional actors for accountability, not as a substitute for access control
guide.summary: The actor directory explains who is responsible for business records. It does not replace principals, memberships, or permission checks.
body: Create functional actors for people or teams that need visible operational accountability.
Link actors to principals when you want traceability between a real sign-in identity and a business-facing ownership role.
Use assignments to show who owns an asset, control, risk, or other governed record.

concepts.label: Functional Actor
concepts.summary: A business-facing person or team entry used to express accountability across governed records.
concepts.why_it_exists: The platform needs stable ownership records even when sign-in identities, teams, or membership models change.
how_to_use: Create actors for roles, named people, or teams that need to appear as owners of business records.
Use actor records to keep governance responsibilities visible without coupling them to login mechanics.

relations.summary: Actors can be assigned to governed records as owners or accountable parties.

concepts.label: Actor to Principal Link
concepts.summary: A link between a functional actor and a sign-in principal.
concepts.why_it_exists: This link preserves the distinction between operational accountability and access control while still allowing them to be related.
how_to_use: Use the link when a functional actor corresponds to a real local or synced principal.
Do not assume the link itself grants permissions.

relations.summary: The link starts from a functional actor.
The link can point to a sign-in principal.

concepts.label: Ownership Assignment
concepts.summary: A link between a functional actor and a business record to express accountability or ownership.
concepts.why_it_exists: The platform needs a way to show who is responsible for something without confusing that responsibility with sign-in access.
how_to_use: Assign the right functional actor to the records that need a visible owner or accountable role.
Do not treat ownership assignments as implicit permission grants.
